violations.text: -Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per interview of PIC, cooked broccoli and roasted slice tomatoes were placed hot (54-61 degrees Fahrenheit) in the 2-door refrigerator in containers covered with plastic wrap. -Per interview of staff broccoli and tomatoes were prepared 1 hour prior to this inspection. Proper cooling methods reviewed. Staff removed broccoli and tomatoes from containers and placed them on to shallow sheet pans to allow for repaid cooling in the walk-in cooler. -Establishment shall keep a cooling log for foods in the cooling process. -Cooling food temp. logs must be completed and provided to inspector (two weeks' worth of logs). -Cooling log provided to establishment. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/04/2025
fly strips observed over the food prep sink. Staff removed fly strips during this inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/04/2025
2 bay sink needs a splash guard separating the food prep sink from hand washing side. New Violation. Correct By: 04/04/2025
Refrigerator door gaskets were observed in poor repair in the salad station. Gaskets shall be repaired or replaced. New Violation. Correct By: 03/18/2025
AC hose observed draining into hand washing sink in the kitchen. Water from AC must not drain into hand washing sink. New Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2025

The ceiling in the kitchen area is in poor repair. some coating observed chipping and peeling from ceiling. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 03/31/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. -Application provided to establishment. New Violation. Correct By: 03/04/2025

Open employees' beverage containers were observed stored above sauté station and on the dry ingredient shelf. Employees shall not drink from open beverage containers in order to prevent contamination of their hands, the container, and exposed food. Staff removed open beverages during this inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/18/2025

Employees were observed washing their hands at the food prep sink. -Proper guidance on where to wash hands were reviewed. -Food employees shall clean their hands in a designated hand-washing sink and not in a food preparation, mop, or ware-washing sink. New Violation. Correct By: 02/18/2025

Raw animal foods (raw beef) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (cheese) in the two-door refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. -PIC relocated raw beef to designated area. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/18/2025

Chlorine sanitizer solution was observed in excess of 200 parts per million (ppm). A chlorine sanitizer solution applied to food contact surfaces must be between 50-100 ppm. Service was contacted. -Establishment will sanitize in the 3 bay sink until dishwasher has been serviced. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/18/2025

The door to the outside, located in the front entrance area, observed not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 03/12/2025
Fly strips observed in the kitchen prep area. Staff removed the fly strip during this inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/18/2025
Refrigerator door gaskets were observed in poor repair in the salad station. Gaskets shall be repaired or replaced. New Violation. Correct By: 03/18/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the areas underneath/behind all kitchen equipment have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. The non-food contact surfaces shall be cleaned as often as necessary to prevent buildup of residue. New Violation. Correct By: 02/25/2025
AC hose observed draining into hand-washing sink in the kitchen. Water from AC must not drain into hand-washing sink. New Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2025

The ceiling in the kitchen area is in poor repair. Some coating observed chipping and peeling from ceiling. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 03/31/2025

The downstairs prep area does not have a hand-washing sink that is conveniently located and/or readily accessible. Hand-washing sinks must be located to allow convenient use by employees in food preparation, food dispensing, and ware-washing areas. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/09/2023

The ceiling in the basement prep area is exposed with wood and pipes that are not smooth, durable, and easily cleanable. The materials must be durable, easily cleanable and nonabsorbent for areas such as food preparation areas. Food shall not be prepped in the area until proper ceiling is installed. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/09/2023
